global_summary: This video documents an expedition led by Julian Bayless, a conservation scientist, to explore and study a pristine rainforest located in a volcanic crater, aiming to understand its ecological value and potential medicinal resources while highlighting the importance of forest conservation.

segments.transcript: Climate change will change habitats.
segments.label: content

segments.transcript: Nothing works in isolation.
segments.label: content

segments.transcript: So if the climate changes, then the forest changes.
segments.label: content

segments.transcript: The plants change, the animals change.
segments.label: content

segments.transcript: When you've got human interference on top of these habitats,
segments.label: content

segments.transcript: it's very difficult to actually distinguish between what is actually relating to climate change
segments.label: content

segments.transcript: and what is actually relating to human interference.
segments.label: content

segments.transcript: So here we have a very unique experience with a forest,
segments.label: content

segments.transcript: which has had no human interference.
segments.label: content

segments.transcript: And so therefore any changes that we find on Mount Lico
segments.label: content

segments.transcript: compared to other forests that we looked at
segments.label: content

segments.transcript: will be down to climate change.
segments.label: content

segments.transcript: To have a good baseline to compare it to,
segments.label: content

segments.transcript: so it would be like this is the carbon that should be in the forest,
segments.label: content

segments.transcript: and then compare that to other sites that are disturbed.
segments.label: content

segments.transcript: We can see effectively what the human influence is

segments.transcript: and add that to the man-made climate change part of the equation
segments.label: content

segments.transcript: because it's not the forest for releasing carbon,
segments.label: content

segments.transcript: it's us causing the forests to release carbon.
